phenotypes.label: Metabolic syndrome with lipoma and depression
phenotypes.description: Metabolic syndrome co-occurs with recurrent mild major depression. Frequent benign lipomatous tumors point to an adiposity-driven phenotype; a subset has chronic opioid dependence.

phenotypes.label: B-cell lymphoid malignancies with cytopenias
phenotypes.description: B‑cell lymphoid cancers dominate, including non‑Hodgkin lymphoma, diffuse large B‑cell lymphoma, chronic lymphocytic leukemia, and multiple myeloma, accompanied by neutropenia, thrombocytopenia, pancytopenia, and hypogammaglobulinemia. Patients show lymphadenopathy, splenomegaly, fever, and infectious or immune complications; inflammatory bowel disease appears in a subset, reflecting broader immune dysregulation.

phenotypes.label: Type 1 diabetes with microalbuminuria
phenotypes.description: Pattern of type 1 diabetes with hyperglycemia, dyslipidemia, and early diabetic kidney disease manifested by microalbuminuria. A smaller subset shows secondary diabetes and pancreatic pathology, suggesting pancreatogenic diabetes in some patients.

phenotypes.label: Sickle cell cardiomyopathy with iron overload
phenotypes.description: Sickle cell anemia (hemoglobin SS) co-occurs with disordered iron metabolism and secondary cardiomyopathy, often progressing to heart failure. The pattern is consistent with transfusion‑related iron overload leading to cardiomyopathy, with intermittent pain, chest or abdominal symptoms.

phenotypes.label: Ehlers-Danlos with aneurysms and valvular disease
phenotypes.description: Heritable connective tissue–vascular pattern centered on Ehlers-Danlos features with arterial aneurysms, intracranial vascular findings, and congenital aortic valve insufficiency. Skin and ocular involvement (dermatitis, chronic skin ulcers, glaucoma/optic atrophy) also appear, with occasional cardiopulmonary sequelae such as hypoxia and pulmonary hypertension.

phenotypes.label: Unused / low-signal topic
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions do not form a single clinical story, mixing abdominal pain and GI symptoms with benign uterine disorders, postoperative states, and various malignancy codes. This reads as a broad baseline blend rather than a distinct, actionable phenotype.

phenotypes.label: Diverticulitis and distal radius fracture
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions combine colonic diverticulitis with a distal radius fracture, plus sporadic thyrotoxicosis. These are unrelated problems from different organ systems and do not form a single clinical pattern.

phenotypes.label: Colorectal cancer and anorectal disorders
phenotypes.description: Colon and rectal malignancies with prominent local anorectal symptoms and postsurgical sequelae. Features include rectal bleeding, hemorrhoids, fissures, fecal incontinence, and presence of colostomy or ileostomy, along with rectal polyps and diverticular disease. This reflects colorectal cancer with local complications and stoma-related conditions within the first year after diagnosis.

phenotypes.label: Wrist joint pain
phenotypes.description: Encounters are centered on wrist joint pain, indicating musculoskeletal complaints localized to the wrist. Common etiologies include overuse syndromes, osteoarthritis, or inflammatory arthropathy, with symptoms of localized pain and reduced function.

phenotypes.label: Multiple sclerosis and neuro-ophthalmic disorders
phenotypes.description: Central nervous system disorders with prominent neuro‑ophthalmic involvement, led by multiple sclerosis with optic neuritis, visual field loss, diplopia, and optic atrophy. Accompanying neurologic manifestations include seizures, headaches, trigeminal neuralgia, and abnormal brain imaging; some patients have intracranial lesions such as meningioma or hydrocephalus contributing to visual impairment.

phenotypes.label: Bipolar disorder and PTSD
phenotypes.description: Psychiatric phenotype centered on bipolar disorder (including bipolar II) with prominent co‑occurring post‑traumatic stress disorder. Severe recurrent depressive episodes with psychotic features and comorbid substance and alcohol use disorders also appear, indicating complex mood and trauma‑related comorbidity.

phenotypes.label: Unused / low-signal topic
phenotypes.description: Conditions center on general breast cancer diagnoses, imaging findings, and postoperative status with common symptoms and comorbidities. This reflects broad baseline documentation rather than a distinct, differentiating clinical pattern.

phenotypes.label: Head and neck cancer complications
phenotypes.description: Swallowing and voice dysfunction with head and neck findings dominate, including dysphagia (oropharyngeal), dysphonia, vocal cord paralysis, laryngeal disorders, neck mass, and xerostomia with salivary dysfunction. This pattern reflects head and neck malignancy with local tumor effects causing airway, speech, and salivary gland impairment, often accompanied by cancer-related pain.

phenotypes.label: Ulcerative colitis with iron-deficiency anemia
phenotypes.description: Ulcerative colitis with chronic gastrointestinal blood loss causing iron‑deficiency anemia. Associated features include pancolitis, diarrhea, Clostridioides difficile colitis, and ileostomy status in some patients. Extraintestinal inflammatory findings such as sacroiliitis/arteritis are also represented.

phenotypes.label: Mixed clinical themes
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions include cutaneous malignancy (melanoma/skin cancer) together with chronic rhinosinusitis and nasal structural disorders, plus immune‑mediated diseases such as psoriasis and systemic sclerosis, and unrelated neurologic disease. These span distinct organ systems without a unifying mechanism, so this does not represent a single coherent phenotype.

phenotypes.label: Thyroid and parathyroid disorders
phenotypes.description: Endocrine neck conditions centered on thyroid disease, including thyroid carcinoma, nodular goiter, autoimmune thyroiditis, and both hypothyroidism and thyrotoxicosis (with features such as lid lag). Postoperative hypothyroidism is common, with frequent parathyroid and calcium disturbances (primary hyperparathyroidism, hyper- or hypocalcemia), osteoporosis, and vitamin D deficiency.

phenotypes.label: Onychomycosis and nail dystrophy
phenotypes.description: A podiatric/dermatologic cluster centered on fungal nail infection (onychomycosis) with nail dystrophy/onychogryposis and related nail disorders, alongside foot callosities and skin texture changes. Diabetic peripheral vascular disease and neuropathy appear as coexisting comorbidities, consistent with foot pathology in patients with impaired circulation.

phenotypes.label: Age-related ocular disease and actinic skin
phenotypes.description: Age-related ophthalmic conditions dominate, including presbyopia, cataracts (nuclear/cortical/secondary), refractive errors, ocular surface disease (dry eye, blepharitis, meibomian dysfunction), and ocular hypertension/glaucoma-related findings. Eyelid disorders and periocular involvement are common. Actinic skin disease, including facial basal cell carcinoma and actinic keratoses, suggests coexisting photodamage in older adults.

phenotypes.label: Hematologic and musculoskeletal mix
phenotypes.description: This combines two unrelated patterns: a hematologic cluster with essential thrombocythemia/thrombocytosis, monoclonal gammopathy, and B‑12–related anemias, alongside a musculoskeletal pain cluster. The latter includes chronic neck and back pain, cervical radiculopathy, myofascial pain, somatic dysfunction, and fibromyalgia, with mood disorder coded in remission. These do not reflect a single clinical pathway.

phenotypes.label: Unused / low-signal topic
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions are generic chronic comorbidities and nonspecific complaints seen broadly across adults (e.g., hypertension, hyperlipidemia, GERD, obesity, sleep apnea, diabetes, anxiety, osteoarthritis). There is no unifying disease mechanism; this reflects a general baseline rather than a distinct, clinically targetable pattern.

phenotypes.label: Mixed neurovascular and dermatologic themes
phenotypes.description: This combines episodes of syncope or loss of consciousness with sinus node dysfunction and ischemic stroke—an acute neurocardiovascular pattern. It also includes unrelated skin and soft-tissue conditions such as varicose veins, herpes simplex/vesicular dermatitis, and keloid/hypertrophic scarring, with additional mood/eating disorders, indicating no single clinical storyline.

phenotypes.label: Peripartum mood and psychosis
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions reflect pregnancy and the postpartum period with prominent postpartum depression and mania/psychotic features (hallucinations, panic/anxiety). Additional elements such as new daily persistent headache and acute posthemorrhagic anemia co-occur in the obstetric/postpartum setting, with occasional perinatal infections noted.

phenotypes.label: Common comorbidity baseline
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions are general chronic comorbidities and nonspecific symptoms such as hypertension, hyperlipidemia, GERD, anxiety/depression, obesity, and musculoskeletal pain. There is no unifying clinical mechanism beyond population baseline morbidity, so this pattern does not define a specific phenotype.

phenotypes.label: Bladder cancer with hematuria and UTI
phenotypes.description: Bladder malignancy with prominent hematuria (gross and microscopic) and lower urinary tract symptoms such as dysuria and urinary retention. Obstructive findings like hydronephrosis and urethral stricture are common, alongside recurrent cystitis/UTIs and occasional kidney stones.

phenotypes.label: Medial meniscus tear
phenotypes.description: Medial meniscus derangement of the knee, typically reflecting a degenerative or traumatic meniscal tear. Often associated with knee pain, swelling, and mechanical symptoms such as locking or catching that impair function.

phenotypes.label: Unused / low-signal topic
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions blend nonspecific acute symptoms (pain, chest pain, dyspnea, nausea/vomiting) with common comorbidities (hypertension, diabetes, GERD) and assorted findings (lymphadenopathy, syncope, pericardial effusion). There is no single clinical theme or mechanism tying these together, so this pattern is not clinically specific.

phenotypes.label: Foot and ankle tendinopathy and osteoarthritis
phenotypes.description: Foot and ankle pain driven by plantar fasciitis, calcaneal spur, Achilles and tibialis tendinopathy/bursitis, with flatfoot and ankle/foot osteoarthritis. This reflects chronic mechanical overuse and degenerative pathology causing heel and midfoot pain.

phenotypes.label: Age-related macular and vitreoretinal disease
phenotypes.description: Cluster of age-related retinal and macular disease featuring nonexudative and exudative age-related macular degeneration, epiretinal membrane, vitreous degeneration/opacities, and retinal hemorrhage. Cataract, primary open‑angle glaucoma, dry eye, chorioretinal scarring, and lattice degeneration appear as coexisting ophthalmic conditions suggestive of vitreoretinal pathology.

phenotypes.label: Prostate cancer with BPH and LUTS
phenotypes.description: Urologic pattern centered on prostate malignancy with coexisting benign prostatic hyperplasia, urinary obstruction, nocturia, frequency/urgency, and retention. Erectile dysfunction, hypogonadism, and elevated PSA findings are common within this group.

phenotypes.label: Low-signal corpus baseline
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions are generic symptoms and common chronic comorbidities (pain, hypertension, hyperlipidemia, diabetes, obesity, nausea/vomiting) rather than a specific clinical pattern. Additional findings span disparate areas (skin lesions and nevi, respiratory complaints, gynecologic and colon cancer terms) without a unifying mechanism.

phenotypes.label: Unused / low-signal topic
phenotypes.description: The listed conditions are common chronic comorbidities—type 2 diabetes, hypertension, dyslipidemia, and chronic kidney disease with proteinuria and anemia—seen broadly across the cohort. This pattern reflects baseline comorbidity rather than a specific, clinically distinct phenotype.

phenotypes.label: Kidney cancer and neuropsychiatric disorders
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions split between renal malignancy/solitary kidney and other kidney disorders, and a separate cluster of ADHD, generalized anxiety, recurrent depression, OCD, and insomnia. These reflect unrelated organ systems without a unifying clinical mechanism, so this is not a cohesive phenotype.

phenotypes.label: PTSD with depression and substance remission
phenotypes.description: Chronic post-traumatic stress disorder with recurrent depression, alongside histories of alcohol and tobacco dependence now in remission. Associated dental disease (partial edentulism) suggests longstanding substance use and poor oral health.

phenotypes.label: Peripheral artery disease and pituitary disorders
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions span severe peripheral and carotid atherosclerosis alongside pituitary adenoma with hypopituitarism and adrenal cortical insufficiency, plus myasthenia-related findings. These represent unrelated clinical pathways rather than a single condition, so the set lacks a unifying clinical theme.

phenotypes.label: Unused / low-signal topic
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions do not define a single clinical pattern. They mix very common symptoms and scattered findings rather than a unified disease theme, so it is not clinically actionable.

phenotypes.label: Mixed clinical themes
phenotypes.description: The leading conditions span nicotine dependence, anemia, electrolyte and osmolality disturbances, acute kidney injury, sepsis, mood disorders, and liver disease findings. There is no single coherent clinical theme tying these together, so this pattern is not clinically actionable.

phenotypes.label: HIV with HPV and hepatitis coinfections
phenotypes.description: HIV infection with frequent coinfections by sexually transmitted pathogens, notably HPV-related anogenital dysplasia (anal and cervical), syphilis, and genital herpes. Viral hepatitis B and C are also common. Serious mental illness and substance use disorders often co-occur in this group.

phenotypes.label: Lung cancer with COPD and metastases
phenotypes.description: Primary lung malignancy, often upper-lobe, with solitary pulmonary nodules and abnormal thoracic imaging, along with pleural effusion, atelectasis, and lymphadenopathy. COPD/emphysema with dyspnea and cough commonly co-occur, and some cases show secondary spread to bone or lung.

phenotypes.label: Unused / low-signal topic
phenotypes.description: This set of conditions does not form a distinct clinical pattern. It blends diverse, commonly coded skin findings without a single unifying disorder.

phenotypes.label: Amnesia and mild cognitive impairment
phenotypes.description: Neurocognitive disorders characterized by amnesia, impaired attention, mild cognitive impairment, and broader cognitive impairment. Neurologic signs such as ataxia and brain disorder codes are present; closed fracture and hematoma likely reflect falls in cognitively impaired patients, with some co-occurring device-related infections in those with implanted hardware.
